body.title: [awaiting_validation] Proof emitter: in-place git-checkout red strategy (fix editable-finder shadowing)
body.description:
The shipped proof emitter materializes red via a git worktree + PYTHONPATH, which the PEP 660 editable finder defeats — so it can't prove unseen_university-namespace things (fails safe by rejecting). Replace the worktree strategy with IN-PLACE git checkout, which works WITH the finder (it points at the working tree).

Mechanism (decision: D-proof-on-close-2026-06-20 'Red-strategy + clean-tree principle RESOLVED'): require clean tree (guard already shipped); impl-paths = `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD` minus the test file; `git checkout HEAD~1 -- <impl-paths>` (test stays at HEAD intention); run red in repo_root; restore via `git checkout HEAD -- <impl-paths>` in a finally + verify clean after. NO stash, NO worktree, NO modes — prove the current committed HEAD, red = HEAD~1.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/proof_emitter.py (replace _git_parent_worktree with in-place strategy; prove() unchanged surface), tests/test_proof_emitter.py (update git tests to in-place; add restore-safety + clean-after assertions)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (n/a); test-plan-or-why-not; prove-what's-load-bearing (this is load-bearing — the emitter's correctness on real code)
**Scope boundary:** IN — swap red mechanism to in-place checkout, keep clean-tree guard + stub-first + emit schema; OUT — the close-gate (separate ticket), branch/origin modes (dropped as premature)
**Test plan:** tmp-git-repo tests run checkout-in-place→red→restore and assert (a) authentic AssertionError red, (b) green at HEAD, (c) working tree CLEAN after restore (no leftover HEAD~1 files), (d) a crash mid-red still restores. Document why the finder-shadowing fix is sound (earlier probe: finder resolves to the working tree, so in-place checkout changes what imports).

BUILT + tests green (commit f628256c), awaiting Akien inspection. In-place git-checkout red replaces worktree; fixes editable-finder shadowing. PROVEN: 17 hermetic tests (real git checkout in-place + real subprocess pytest; M happy-path authentic red; A added-file -> correct stub-first rejection [the advisor's silent-hole, now closed]; D; tracked-clean-after-restore; restore-raises-on-dirty). Editable-finder defeat confirmed by direct probe on the REAL package (in-place mutation -> fresh subprocess import sees it -> git checkout restore clean). HONEST GAP: the full composition prove()->_git_inplace_red on a COMMITTED package change isn't a single test (it's covered by orchestration tests + the finder probe); the first real close-gate proof exercises it end-to-end. Caveats documented: shared-tree mutation window; HEAD~1 = weak red for multi-commit things; added impl files need stub-first.
